metadata.source: Meridian artifacts (MRL, Control List, CSC-01, CSP SOC 2 report)

controls.title: New Hire Induction
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to sign the following documents at the time of joining to acknowledge the acceptance of terms to maintain the confidentiality and integrity of information within the organization:
1. Appointment Letter consisting of Code of Ethics and Business Conduct (COBC), and
2. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA).
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the Appointment Letter and Non Disclosure Agreement (NDA) acknowledgement to determine whether the users had signed and agreed to preserve ethical values and the confidentiality and integrity of information within Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of new joiners from Workday for the audit period
For a sample of new joiners, Appointment Letter consisting of Code of Ethics and Business Conduct (COBC)
For a sample of new joiners, Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)

controls.title: Employee Handbook
controls.descriptionText: Formalized employee handbook is documented to communicate expected workforce conduct, organizational policies, and associated enforcement procedures. This document is published on U-Central and accessible by all users.
controls.testProcedure:
Performed inquiry with Senior HR Operations Specialist to determine whether formalized employee handbook was published on U-Central and accessible by all users.

Additionally, inspected the employee handbook to determine whether it communicated expected workforce conduct, organizational policies, and associated enforcement procedures.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from U-Central showing that employee handbook was published on U-Central and is accessible by all users.
Employee Handbook

controls.title: Background and Reference Checks
controls.descriptionText:
The Human Resource team is responsible for carrying out background checks for new hires before the date of hire with the help of a third-party vendor, who conducts these checks and provides a background check report to Meridian. The following background checks are performed:
► International Criminal Searches,
► International Global Watchlist,
► International Education Verification, and
► International Employment Verification.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the background verification reports to determine whether background check was performed before the date of hire for parameters such as International Criminal Searches, International Global Watchlist, International Education Verification, and International Employment Verification.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of new joiners, BGV reports.

controls.title: Technical Interviews
controls.descriptionText: New hires are required to undergo technical interviews prior to appointment to help ensure that skill sets match with job requirements.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the technical interview assessment forms to determine whether the skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of new joiners for Bangalore & Chennai locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

For a sample of new joiners for London and Valencia locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

For a sample of new joiners for Palo Alto and New York locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

controls.title: User Trainings - New Hires
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to attend the following virtual trainings on Mindtickle platform during induction containing elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches:
1. Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct, and
2. Information Security and Compliance.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and Information Security and Compliance training material to determine whether it contained elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.

Additionally, for a sample of new hires, inspected the training completion records within Mindtickle to determine whether the new hires were made aware of code of conduct and information security and compliance during induction.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and Information Security and Compliance training material
For a sample of new joiners, the training completion records within Mindtickle

controls.title: User Trainings - Active Users
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, all active users are required to attend the following virtual Information Security and Compliance refresher trainings on Mindtickle platform, which includes information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the refresher Information Security and Compliance training material to determine whether it contained elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.

Additionally, for a sample of active users, inspected the training completion records within Mindtickle to determine whether the active users completed the refresher trainings.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of active users from Workday as on date
Refresher Information Security and Compliance training material for active users
For a sample of active users, the training completion records within Mindtickle

controls.title: Performance Evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Performance evaluations are carried out on a bi-annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six months. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of active employees who have been in Meridian for more than six months, inspected the performance evaluation results within Workday to determine whether performance evaluations were carried out by employees and their reporting managers.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of active users, performance evaluation results within Workday

controls.title: VCSOM evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Vision, Culture, Strategy, Operating Model (VCSOM) evaluations are carried out on an annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six months. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out VCSOM evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of active employees who have been in Meridian for more than six months, inspected the VCSOM evaluation results within Workday to determine whether VCSOM evaluations were carried out by employees and their reporting managers.

controls.title: Organization Chart
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has established an organizational structure to define reporting lines, authorities, and responsibilities for NXSC to meet its objectives for providing Software as a Service (SaaS) services to its user entities.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Meridian organizational chart to determine whether Meridian has defined an organizational structure which describes the line of authority and the individual roles and responsibilities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Meridian's organizational chart

controls.title: User job roles and responsibilities
controls.descriptionText: Roles and responsibilities are defined in written job descriptions to assign responsibility for security, confidentiality, availability, and processing integrity.
controls.testProcedure:
Inquired the Information Security Senior Manager to determine whether the Job Descriptions were updated within Workday and reflect the current job responsibilities.

Additionally, for a sample of Job Descriptions, inspected the job description to determine whether roles and responsibilities were defined.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of Job Descriptions, job description document showing that roles and responsibilities were defined.

controls.title: Risk Management Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a Risk Management Procedure that contains guidelines for the identification, preparation, and categorization of risks to satisfy business information, legal and regulatory requirements. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Risk Management Procedure to determine whether guidelines for the identification, preparation, and categorization of risks to satisfy business information, legal and regulatory requirements were defined and documented.

Additionally, inspected U-Central to determine whether the Risk Management Procedure was published on U-Central and was reviewed and approved by CISO.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Risk Management Procedure document
Screenshot from U-Central showing that Risk Management Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Annual Risk Assessment
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance team performs risk assessment of the various functions within Meridian with the support of internal Meridian stakeholders and prepares a risk register. Risks are identified based on inherent risk value, internal and external threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and potential impact to Meridian’s objectives, assets, and operations. Risks are evaluated based on impact magnitude and expectancy criteria in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability. Mitigating controls are identified to bring the inherent risk to acceptable levels and is documented within a Jira ticket.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Risk Register and corresponding Jira ticket(s) to determine whether the Information Security & Compliance team identified, reviewed, and documented the risks associated with its various functions in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest Risk Register

Jira ticket(s) showing the Information Security & Compliance team identified, reviewed, and documented the risks associated with its various functions in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.

controls.title: Vendor Risk Management - Onboarding Assessments
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance Team performs vendor onboarding assessments for new vendors and third-party service providers as part of its vendor risk management process. The Information Security & Compliance team requests independent compliance and security related certifications to assess whether the vendor meets the third-party information security requirements defined by Meridian.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of vendors and third party service providers onboarded during the audit period, inspected the onboarding assessment report to determine whether Information Security & Compliance team assessed whether the vendor meets the third-party information security requirements defined by Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of vendors and third party service providers onboarded during the audit period from Streamline
For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, vendor onboarding assessment reports

controls.title: Management Review Meetings (MRM)
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, Management Review Meetings (MRM) are conducted amongst the Executive Management, Steering Committee (SSC), CISO, Legal team, Privacy team, and the  Information Security & Compliance teams to ensure security initiatives are in line with company objectives and to discuss non-conformities and findings identified during information security internal audits / risk assessments / any other assessments, incidents, and their respective corrective actions were performed.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Management Review Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes to determine whether the Executive Management, Steering Committee (SSC), CISO, Legal team, Privacy team, and the  Information Security & Compliance teams met annually to ensure security initiatives were in line with company objectives and discussed nonconformities and findings identified during information security internal audits / risk assessments / other assessments, incidents, and their respective corrective actions.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual Management Review Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes of the meeting

controls.title: Board of Directors Independence
controls.descriptionText: Meridian operates under the direction of its Board of Directors. The Board of Directors comprises of members independent from Meridian’s daily operations.
controls.testProcedure:
Performed inquiry with Meridian CISO to determine whether Board of Directors constituted of members independent of Meridian daily operations.

Additionally, inspected the ‘List of Directors’ and Board of Directors structure of Meridian to determine whether the Board of Directors demonstrated independence from Meridian's daily operations.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of Board of Directors

controls.title: Board of Directors Meeting
controls.descriptionText: On a quarterly basis, matters related to Meridian’s results of operations, cybersecurity updates, projections for the coming quarter, and any major changes, are discussed between the Board of Directors and key members of management.
controls.testProcedure: For sample of quarters inspected the Board of Directors Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes to determine whether matters related to Meridian’s results of operations, projections for the coming quarter, and any major changes were discussed between Board of Directors and key members of management.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of quarters, Board of Directors Meeting invites, agenda, and minutes of the meeting

controls.title: Information Security Internal Audit
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, an information security management system related internal audit is conducted to assess the operating effectiveness of controls related to information security and information technology. This audit is conducted for Quanta Capture Legacy, User Access Management, Information Security, Network Security, Endpoint Security, Physical Security, and Human Resource Security. Observations or issues identified during the audit are documented in the internal audit report along with recommendations for resolutions of the observations or issues and a management response is sought for action plans.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Internal Audit Report to determine whether information security and information technology related internal audit was conducted to assess the operating effectiveness of controls related to information security and information technology for Quanta Capture Legacy, User Access Management, Information Security, Data Privacy, Network Security, Endpoint Security, Physical Security, and Human Resource Security.

Additionally, inspected the relevant Jira tickets to determine whether observations were tracked to closure.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Internal Audit Report for the audit period
Email communication showing that internal audit report is shared with the respective stakeholders.

controls.title: Subservice Organization Assessments
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Information Security & Compliance team assesses the subservice organization’s compliance with the applicable security, confidentiality, availability, and processing integrity commitments; and evaluates the impact of any noted exceptions of relevant controls and Complementary User Entity Controls by obtaining and evaluating their annual System and Organization Control attestation report.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Subservice Organization Assessment report to determine whether the GRC team had assessed the subservice organizations’ compliance with security, confidentiality, availability, processing integrity, and privacy commitments by evaluating their System and Organization Control attestation report.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Subservice Organization Assessment report for AWS
Subservice Organization Assessment report for Freshworks
Subservice Organization Assessment report for ReliaQuest
Subservice Organization Assessment report for Microsoft Speech Services
Subservice Organization Assessment report for Duplo Cloud

controls.title: Vendor Risk Management - Annual Re-assessments
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, the Information Security & Compliance Team performs a re-assessment for all vendors and third-party service providers as part of its vendor risk management process. Observations identified during the assessment are communicated to the third-party service providers and are tracked to closure by the Information Security & Compliance team.
controls.testProcedure:
For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, inspected the re-assessment report to determine whether Information Security & Compliance team assessed whether the vendor meets the third-party information security requirements defined by Meridian.

Additionally, inspected the relevant Jira tickets to determine whether observations were tracked to closure.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of all active vendors and third party service providers as on date from Streamline
For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, re-assessment report
Jira tickets showing observations were tracked to closure.

controls.title: ISO 27001
controls.descriptionText:
2022 and PCI DSS v4.0:
On an annual basis, assessment under standard ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and PCI DSS v4.0 are performed by an external certification body.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the ISO 27001:2022 and PCI DSS v4.0 certificates to determine whether Meridian was certified by external certification bodies.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest ISO 27001:2022 certificate
Latest PCI DSS v4.0 certificate

controls.title: Information Security Awareness
controls.descriptionText: On a monthly basis, the Information Security and Compliance team sends mass emails to all users on information security awareness updates and leading practices in order to spread awareness.

evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of months, email communication showing all users were made aware about information security awareness updates and leading practices.

controls.title: MSA - User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian defines its terms of service; security obligations; intellectual property; availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity commitments; and compliance with laws within its Master Software and Services Agreements (MSAs) that are signed with user entities during onboarding.

controls.title: MSA / SOW - Vendors

controls.title: Software Development Lifecycle (SDLC) Procedure
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a SDLC Procedure that contains guidelines on best practices for training and implementation of software security during application development to maximise their inherent security. It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.

Inspected the Software Development Lifecycle Procedure to determine whether guidelines on best practices for training and implementation of software security during application development to maximise their inherent security were defined and documented.

Additionally, inspected U-Central to determine whether the Software Development Lifecycle Procedure was published on Quanta Capture and was reviewed and approved by CISO.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Software Development Lifecycle Procedure maintained for Quanta Capture Legacy product
Screenshot from U-Central showing Software Development Lifecycle Procedure was published on U-Central

controls.title: Global Information Security Policy
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has defined and developed the Global Information Security Policy addressing matters related to system security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity principles.

Additionally, the following are also defined:
► Meridian's objectives pertaining to integrity and ethical values
 i. Code of Business Conduct
 ii. Role Competencies
 iii. Disciplinary Policies
► Human Resources security policy for hiring, termination, and periodic performance appraisals of users,
► Clear desk and clear screen policy,
► Media Handling Guidelines for electronic and paper based media disposal,
► Access Control Policies for user access management, privilege management, and password management, and
► Physical Security Policy for physical security and environmental safeguards.

It is published on U-Central and is reviewed and approved by the CISO on an annual basis.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Global Information Security Policy to determine whether system security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity principles; code of business conduct, role competencies, disciplinary policies, human resources security policy, clear desk and clear screen policy, media handling guidelines, access control policies, and physical security policies were defined and documented.

Additionally, inspected U-Central to determine whether the Global Information Security Policy was published on U-Central and was reviewed and approved annually by CISO.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Global Information Security Policy
Screenshot from U-Central showing Global Information Security Policy was published on U-Central

controls.title: Access Creation - IT accounts
controls.descriptionText: When a user joins the organization, HR team creates a user ID within Workday HRMS tool, which automatically sends an email communication to the IT team and Workspace Experience team and creates an account within the local Azure Active Directory.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory to determine whether an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically created when a user ID is created within Workday HRMS tool.

Additionally, for a sample of new joiners, inspected the automated Workday emails and access creation logs within Azure Active Directory to determine whether the local Azure Active Directory accounts were created.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory that an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically created when a user ID is created within Workday HRMS tool.
Screenshot showing configuration within Workday that the global onboarded email is sent automatically to the IT and Workplace team on the date of joining of the employee.
For a sample of new joiners, automated Workday emails containing employee's date of joining was shared with the IT team requesting to create the AD accounts.
For a sample of new joiners,email communication showing that IT team confirmed that the access to Azure AD account is created.
For a sample of new joiners, screenshot from Azure Active Directory showing the access creation date.

controls.title: Access Revocation - IT accounts
controls.descriptionText: On the last working day of a user, whether resignation, termination, or absconding, HR team deactivates the user ID within Workday HRMS tool, which automatically deactivates the user's account within the local Azure Active Directory. The IT team validates the access revocation of the user on the last working day and confirms the same via email.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory to determine whether an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically deactivated when a user ID is deactivated within Workday HRMS tool.

Additionally, for a sample of leavers, inspected the access revocation logs within Azure Active Directory to determine whether the user's access was revoked on or before their last working date.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing configuration within Workday HRMS and Azure Active Directory that an account within the local Azure Active Directory is automatically deactivated when a user ID is deactivated within Workday HRMS tool.
Screenshot showing configuration within Workday that the global offboarding email is sent automatically to the IT team on the last working date of the employee.
For a sample of leavers, automated Workday emails containing employee's date of joining was shared with the IT team requesting to deactivate the AD accounts.
For a sample of leavers, email communication showing that IT team confirmed that the access to Azure AD account is deactivated.
For a sample of new joiners, screenshot from Azure Active Directory showing the access deactivation date.

controls.title: Access Review - IT Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance team performs a review of the administrative access to firewalls and Okta on a quarterly basis. Users along with their privileges are reviewed with the help of CISO within a Jira ticket. In case of any discrepancies, the respective team takes necessary corrective actions. The CISO reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.

controls.title: Password and Account Lockout Policy – Active Directory
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has implemented Azure Active Directory to enforce password and account lockout policy settings on end user workstations. These policy settings include password length as 12 characters, password complexity, password history as 24 passwords remembered, and password expiry as 90 days. Multi-factor authentication is enforced for all users added to the Azure Active Directory. User accounts are locked after 5 invalid logon attempts and account lockout is reset after 30 minutes through group policy settings.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the group policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory to determine whether password settings included configurations to enforce password length as 12 characters, password complexity, password history as 24 passwords remembered, and password expiry as 90 days, account lockout after 5 invalid logon attempts, and account lockout is reset after 30 minutes through group policy settings.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of Password policy settings configured on Meridian Azure Active Directory

controls.title: Idle Session Timeout - Active Directory

controls.title: Firewall Rules
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has implemented perimeter firewalls on the Bengaluru, Chennai, London, Palo Alto, New York, and Valencia delivery center internet gateways to proactively identify and protect Meridian network against security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity threats from sources outside the system boundaries. Rules are enforced on the firewalls to:
► ‘deny all’ traffic by default with access enabled for specific services as per business requirements,
► protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks, and
► configure Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters to monitor and prevent intrusion attempts.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the management approved network diagrams of Bengaluru, Chennai, London, Palo Alto, New York, and Valencia to determine whether firewall was implemented on the delivery center's internet gateway.

Additionally, inspected the configurations within the firewalls to determine whether rules were configured and enforced to:
► ‘deny all’ traffic by default with access enabled for specific services as per business requirements,
► protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks, and
► configure Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters to monitor and prevent intrusion attempts.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Management approved network diagrams for all in-scope locations

controls.title: Source-Code Reviews
controls.descriptionText: Meridian conducts annual source code reviews through a third-party vendor to identify security vulnerabilities for the repositories related to the X Platform and the Quanta Portfolio products. The Information Security team raises JIRA tickets for high and critical severity issues, and the Engineering and Infrastructure Operations teams implement corrective actions within the defined SLAs. A follow-up remediation review is performed by the vendor to ensure issues are resolved, reinforcing Meridian's commitment to secure coding practices and data protection.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the annual source-code review report for Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products to determine whether security vulnerabilities for the repositories were identified and documented.

Further, inspected the JIRA tickets for high and critical issues to determine whether the Engineering and Infrastructure Operations teams implement corrective actions within the defined SLAs. 

Additionally, inspected the email communication to determine whether A follow-up remediation review is performed by the vendor to ensure issues are resolved, reinforcing Meridian's commitment to secure coding practices and data protection.
evidenceRequirementTexts: JIRA tickets for high and critical issues identified in the source-code review reports.

controls.title: Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, third-party vendors conduct application level, network level, and API vulnerability assessments and penetration tests for Quanta Capture Legacy product. Based on the findings of these tests, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team follows up with the relevant stakeholders on appropriate corrective and preventive actions for identified gaps until closure of the gaps. Based on the confirmation received from the relevant stakeholders, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team notifies the third-party vendor to perform rescan to confirm the closure of gaps through email. On completion of rescan, third-party vendor shares the summary report (Compliant Report) with the Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the annual vulnerability assessment and penetration test reports for Quanta Capture Legacy product to determine whether application level, network level, and API vulnerability and penetration tests for Quanta Capture were conducted, and corrective and preventive actions were tracked to closure for identified vulnerabilities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual Vulnerability assessment and penetration test (VAPT) reports for all Quanta Capture Legacy product
Evidence showing that corrective and preventive actions were tracked to closure for identified vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Visitor Management
controls.descriptionText: Security guards are responsible for ensuring that details of the visitors or vendors, such as name of the visitor or vendor, contact details, contact person in Meridian, entry time and exit time are recorded within the Visitor Register maintained at the reception area prior to permitting entry to the delivery center. Visitors and vendors are issued a visitor identification card, which they are required to display while inside the delivery center. An authorized employee escorts visitors or vendors during their visit to the delivery center.
controls.testProcedure:
Through the use of video streaming technology assisted by Meridian Facility team, inspected the Meridian Delivery Centers to determine whether security guards supervised the details entered by the visitors or vendors such as name of the visitor or vendor, purpose of visit, contact person, and entry / exit time, within the visitor register.

controls.title: Physical Access Reconciliation
controls.descriptionText: Physical access to Meridian Delivery Centers is reconciled on a quarterly basis by the Workspace Experience team. The Workspace Experience team extracts the list of active users that have access to Meridian delivery centers from the access control software of that delivery center and compares this list with the list of active users shared by HR team. In case of any discrepancies, the Workspace Experience team takes necessary corrective actions and shares the results of the review with the Information Security & Compliance team.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of quarters, inspected the access reconciliation email communication between Workspace Experience team and the Information Security & Compliance team to determine whether a reconciliation of the personnel with access to the Meridian Delivery Centers and the list of active users was performed.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of quarters, email communication showing reconciliation of the personnel with access to the Meridian Delivery Centers (Bangalore location) and the list of active users is performed.

controls.title: Access Review - Physical Security Tools
controls.descriptionText: Administrative access to Access Control Software is reviewed by the Workspace Experience team on a six-monthly basis. Personnel from the Workspace Experience team extracts the list of administrators on Access Control Software and shares it with Manager – Workspace Experience. The Manager – Workspace Experience reviews the list of administrators to determine the appropriateness of access based on the job responsibilities and communicates discrepancies, if any, to the Workspace Experience team. In case of any discrepancies, the Workspace Experience team takes necessary corrective actions and shares confirmation to the Manager – Workspace Experience. The Manager – Workspace Experience provides sign off over email once the activity has concluded.
